title: lindsaea novoguineensis
intro: Lindsaea novoguineensis, also known as the New Guinea tree fern, is a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. It is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Papua New Guinea and other parts of Asia, where it is valued for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, skin infections, and digestive problems, due to its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Lindsaea novoguineensis is often made into a tea or tincture, and it can also be dried and used in traditional remedies, such as poultices and infusions.
